title: The Babees Book and selected manners-and-meals texts (AHA6127 edited anthology)
summary: The checked-in artifact is a byte-identical copy of University of Michigan CME item AHA6127.0001.001 pinned through the CME repository at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c. The electronic item derives from Frederick J. Furnivall's 1868 edited anthology but its selected body is not one medieval work: twenty-nine edition-level parts resolve into thirty-seven intellectual items, including parallel versions, ten separately modeled recipes, three Dietary-group works, and seven Rawlinson counsel items. Furnivall's selected texts and apparatus draw on British Library, Lambeth Palace Library, Bodleian, Trinity College Cambridge, and Jesus College Cambridge witnesses; later or collateral editions by Thomas Austin and Wright and Halliwell resolve particular source questions. Complete provider-exposed public image bundles are cached for Bodleian MS Ashmole 61, Bodleian MS Rawl. C. 86, Trinity O.9.38, and Trinity R.3.19; Lambeth exposes only selected images, and no currently retrievable complete public facsimile was verified for the other principal witnesses. Access, image supply, copyright, license, and contract terms remain separate assertions.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is an edited selective anthology, not one medieval work, one authorial collection, one witness, one edition, one encoding, or one repository file.

The edition-level boundary is twenty-nine selected parts; the intellectual count is thirty-seven. Parallel ABC, Children's Maxims, and Stans versions count once per family, while their selected versions remain explicit zero-count children.
The 3,603 non-note L count and minimally normalized 3,607-line representation are representation metrics only; aggregate abstract-work verse_lines and verse_groups remain null.

All fifty-four files under source-cache/aha6127 are modeled below with exact source URLs, checksums, byte counts, dates, coverage, and explicit target-work presence. Metadata, service, terms, inventory, and scan-structure files are supporting and marked absent even when they describe the target.
Complete IIIF ZIPs are complete provider-exposed surface inventories at the requested access profile, not claims of archival masters or every physical side beyond what the provider manifest exposes.

For inaccessible witnesses, the planned whole-object-first acquisition route begins by asking whether an existing complete surrogate can be reactivated or supplied; only then would it request a full-color whole-object reproduction with boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, endleaves, blanks, stubs, and every recto and verso. No enquiry has been submitted, and target-only images are a fallback.

The cached CME repository LICENSE contains software-worded Unlicense text. Its scope over inherited U-M transcription/XML data, medieval text, and edition content is unresolved and is not broadened beyond rights held by the affirmer; U-M's current NoC-US assertion and the embedded legacy XML availability warning are recorded separately.

Furnivall's Harley 276 heading for Fretoure is erroneous. The text is in Harley 279, f. 34v, Vyaunde Leche no. 54, confirmed through Austin's direct edition; Harley 276 is evidence of the error, never a target witness.

Current and legacy locators are not silently conflated: Urbanitatis current ff. 88r-v versus Furnivall fol. 86; Jesus current Q.G.8 versus legacy Q.T.8; and Harley 5401's printed-page/folio pairs remain dual un-equated locators.

entities.label: General Corpus repository artifact aha6127.xml

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic item AHA6127.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 OCR-based Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries electronic representation of Furnivall's publication. Its selected BODY stream is the edited anthology boundary cataloged here.
entities.reproduction_format: OCR text with Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries-style XML markup and page-image references

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall, The Babees Book and related texts (1868)
entities.description: The immediate print source of the electronic encoding and the editorial layer that selects, titles, arranges, collates, and annotates the heterogeneous target texts.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., The Babees Book, Aristotle's A B C, Urbanitatis, Stans Puer ad Mensam, and related texts, EETS o.s. 32 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1868).
bibliographic.title: The Babees Book
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 32
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive/Getty scan of Furnivall 1868
entities.description: A complete 694-page scan carrier with PDF, OCR, page-number map, scandata, and item metadata cached for direct source verification.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, page-number JSON, scandata XML, and item metadata JSON

entities.label: Thomas Austin, Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books (1888)
entities.description: A later direct edition of Harley 279 used to identify Fretoure at f. 34v, Vyaunde Leche no. 54, and correct Furnivall's Harley 276 heading. It is supporting evidence, not an ancestor of the 1868 edition.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Austin, ed., Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books, EETS o.s. 91 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1888).
bibliographic.title: Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books

entities.description: Complete PDF and OCR carrier with item metadata.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, and item metadata JSON

entities.label: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, volume 1 (1845)
entities.description: A historical edition consulted for the Jesus Q.G.8 Stans opening and variants at pp. 156–158 and the Lansdowne Dogg Lardyner and Maxims texts at p. 233.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845).
bibliographic.title: Reliquiae Antiquae

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Reliquiae Antiquae, volume 1
entities.description: Complete PDF carrier and scan-structure XML.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records relevant to the AHA6127 Dietary and Rawlinson groups
entities.description: Versioned bibliographic-work and witness records for 5964.5, 2369, 4810, 6137, 6242, 1333, 1867, 1112, and 2745. Exact allLines-bearing selected witness loci are recorded in the local copy.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: Urbanitatis at current ff. 88r–88v in the component record for ff. 3–139. Furnivall's fol. 86 is a legacy locator, not silently equated with current foliation.

entities.label: British Library, Egerton MS 1995
entities.description: Collateral source for bracketed supplies and variants in The Lytylle Childrenes Lytil Boke, ff. 58v–60r.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2251
entities.description: Principal surviving source for Furnivall's first selected Stans version at ff. 148r–149r; its opening is missing and supplied from Jesus Q.G.8 through Wright and Halliwell.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 276 record (refuted Fretoure source)
entities.description: Current catalog evidence that Harley 276 is a thirteenth-century Gospel book, not the Fretoure witness. Select illuminated-image coverage, if any, is irrelevant to the target recipe.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 279
entities.description: Principal source for five selected recipes at ff. 15r, 16r, 39v–40r, and 43v, and the corrected Fretoure source at f. 34v, Vyaunde Leche no. 54.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 4011
entities.description: Source of the selected Boke of Nurture at ff. 171r–189v; the current catalog qualifies the text as here attributed to John Russell.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 5086
entities.description: Source of The Babees Book at ff. 86r–90r and Lerne or be Lewde at f. 90v.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 5401
entities.description: Source cited for four selected prose recipes. Furnivall supplies dual printed-page and folio-style locators that are preserved without asserting they are equivalent pagination systems.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 541
entities.description: Base witness for The Lytylle Childrenes Lytil Boke at ff. 210r–210v.

entities.label: British Library, Lansdowne MS 762
entities.description: Source at f. 16v for the Maxims in -ly and a comparison text of A Dogg Lardyner, printed by Wright and Halliwell on p. 233.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 1986
entities.description: Source for The Boke of Curtasye at ff. 12r–27v and Household Statutes at ff. 100r–102v; Furnivall's legacy pages 193–197 are retained separately.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853
entities.description: A 119-folio vellum manuscript containing the selected ABC at pp. 30–32, Good Wife at pp. 102–112, Stans at pp. 150–155, Manners at pp. 155–157, Dietary group at pp. 182–185, and Wise Man at pp. 186–193. The current catalog summary says fourteenth century while reporting Carleton Brown's fifteenth-century view; those date layers remain distinct.

historical_designations: World Microfilms, Lambeth Palace Library: The Mediaeval Manuscripts, Section I, Reel 15
holding.notes: The two microfilm references are reported access/surrogate routes; complete frame coverage has not yet been verified.

notes: Old binding rebacked; parchment over wooden boards.

entities.label: Lambeth LUNA selected images overlapping MS 853 target texts
entities.description: Five public single-image selections: ABC pp. 30–31; Good Wife pp. 102, 105, and 107; and the tail of Manners at p. 157. They are partial selections, not a complete codex facsimile.

entities.label: Jesus College Cambridge MS Q.G.8
entities.description: A Jesus College manuscript containing the collateral Stans source at ff. 77r–78v. Q.T.8 is the legacy printed shelfmark; Q.G.8 is the current catalog shelfmark.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: Source of The Young Children's Book at f. 20r–v.

notes: record-2369 and record-6137 are target-relevant metadata/full-work records but their selected witnesses do not provide full allLines text; the Latin couplet is represented by the Bodleian catalog, Walter 18075.
The selected record-4810 witness stores its complete eight-line text in firstLines rather than allLines, so the exact field name is preserved rather than normalized.

The complete v1.0 release is cached and passed ZIP integrity and XML validation; the annotated tag object is 74d7c4890f4f7a5c01990997ddd0b5cbd9e7be41 and peels to commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c. It is not a hand-exported record subset.

access.access_method: Ask whether the previously digitized complete Harley 2251, Harley 4011, Harley 5086, and Lansdowne 762 objects can be reactivated or supplied.
access.format: Legacy complete digital objects currently unavailable
notes: Unavailable is not equivalent to never digitized. Request reactivation or supply of each existing whole object before commissioning new photography.

access.access_method: Exact records, Digitised Content fields, current digitised-manuscripts list, and public discovery routes checked for Cotton Caligula A II, Egerton 1995, Harley 279, Harley 5401, Harley 541, and Sloane 1986.
notes: This is a dated negative public-access finding, not proof that unpublished, internal, selected, or privately supplied photography does not exist.
Harley 276 is excluded because it is not a true target witness.

access.access_method: Request each true source shelfmark with a valid Reader Pass and consult manuscripts and archives in London.

First ask for reactivation or supply of any existing complete surrogate; if unavailable, request a preservation-approved full-color whole-object reproduction, with target leaves separately identified.
access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions or licensing staff for written reuse terms
access.format: Manuscript photography subject to preservation assessment
notes: Define whole object as covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, every flyleaf and endleaf, blanks, stubs, detached matter, and both sides of every folio in the current object; ask staff to confirm full foliation and image inventory.
State target leaves independently. A target-only order is a partial fallback, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
Image delivery does not itself grant republication or redistribution rights; obtain written permission and exact use-specific terms separately.

notes: No public images covering the complete Stans, Dietary, Wise Man, or full ABC, Good Wife, and Manners targets were verified. This dated finding does not exclude internal or requestable surrogates.

access.access_method: First verify whether MS Film 1232 or World Microfilms Section I Reel 15 provides complete frame coverage and whether Lambeth can supply or digitize that existing surrogate.
notes: Do not call either reported microfilm complete until frame-by-frame physical coverage is confirmed.

access.access_method: If no complete existing surrogate can be supplied, request a full-color whole-codex project; use the posted limited-image service or target-only pages only as fallback.
access.format: Existing surrogate supply or new manuscript photography
notes: Define whole codex as boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, endleaves, blanks, stubs, every recto and verso, and detached matter; ask staff to confirm inventory.
Identify target pp. 30–32, 102–112, 150–157, and 182–193 separately.
The posted 40-image route or target-only order is partial fallback coverage.
Image supply, permission to reproduce, copyright, and contract conditions are separate; obtain written reuse terms.

access.access_method: Use the exact Trinity manuscript record and Wren Library reader guidance to arrange consultation of the original; the separate complete public IIIF surrogate should normally be consulted first.

rights.component: Underlying medieval texts represented in the selected anthology
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom; general age-based analysis
rights.basis: The medieval textual works predate modern copyright terms by centuries. This does not determine rights in modern transcriptions, encodings, images, or provider contracts.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1868 publication text and editorial matter
rights.basis: Published in 1868; the historical edition is public domain by age. This statement does not license modern scan files or platform metadata.

rights.component: Austin's 1888 publication text and editorial matter
rights.basis: Published in 1888; public domain by age. This statement does not license modern scan files or platform metadata.

rights.component: Wright and Halliwell's 1845 publication text and editorial matter
rights.basis: Published in 1845; public domain by age. This statement does not license modern scan files or platform metadata.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files, OCR, and metadata for the Furnivall 1868 edition
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive item metadata supplies no item-specific license. Public access and public-domain underlying editions do not automatically settle rights in every scan derivative or metadata component.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files, OCR, and metadata for the Austin 1888 edition
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive item metadata supplies no item-specific license. Public access and a public-domain underlying edition do not automatically settle rights in every scan derivative or metadata component.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and scan metadata for the Wright-Halliwell 1845 edition
rights.basis: The cached scan-structure metadata supplies no item-specific license. Public access and a public-domain underlying edition do not automatically settle rights in every scan derivative or metadata component.

rights.component: The checked-in repository XML artifact and the pinned CME repository LICENSE text's unresolved relationship to inherited U-M transcription/XML data, medieval text, and Furnivall edition content
rights.basis: The pinned repository contains an Unlicense-form LICENSE expressly worded for software. The reviewed evidence does not establish that its affirmer held or intended to waive every right in inherited U-M transcription/XML data, medieval text, or edition content; no waiver is broadened beyond the affirmer's rights.
notes: No definitive Unlicense is assigned to the inherited U-M encoding, transcription/XML data, medieval text, or edition content in this manifest.

rights.component: The current U-M digital item and encoding in the United States
rights.basis: The current official U-M CME collection page states that the collection materials are public domain in the United States and assigns the RightsStatements.org No Copyright–United States designation.
notes: The NoC-US designation is territorially limited to the United States.

The embedded legacy XML availability statement separately warns that copyright applies where applicable and that other distribution restrictions may apply. That dated warning is retained and is not silently canceled by the current label.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Heterogeneous medieval conduct, household, recipe, and counsel texts drawn from many witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: An edited anthology with selected texts, omitted divisions, supplied readings, alternating parallel versions, and apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's publication-layer selection is the immediate source of the electronic item's boundaries; it does not create one medieval intellectual work.
editorial_practices.scope: AHA6127 selected print pages and XML BODY

editorial_practices.source_feature: Parallel ABC, Children's Maxims, and Stans versions plus Good Wife and Russell comparison readings
editorial_practices.representation: Alternating-page versions, bracketed supplies, and note apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Parallel witnesses are kept as version layers rather than double-counted abstract works; fifty-three L elements inside notes are excluded from the selected-text 3,603-L count.
editorial_practices.scope: ABC pp. 9–12; Children's Maxims pp. 16–25; Stans pp. 26–33; Good Wife pp. 36–47; Russell pp. 117–199

editorial_practices.source_feature: Furnivall heading Harl. MS. 276 versus the text in Harley 279
editorial_practices.representation: The inherited erroneous heading remains visible, while lineage identifies Harley 279 f. 34v, Vyaunde Leche no. 54 as the actual source
editorial_practices.description: Austin's direct later edition and current catalog records refute Harley 276 without silently altering the historical source text.

open_questions.question: Should DIMEV 5964.5 remain a countable work or be reclassified as witness-specific paratext to Lydgate's Dietary?
next_steps: Review DIMEV's full editorial rationale and every known witness.
Preserve both its separate bibliographic record and its rubric-like function until stronger evidence resolves the boundary.

open_questions.question: Should any of the seven Rawlinson f. 31r items be grouped as one historical compilation rather than seven intellectual items?
next_steps: Retain DIMEV 6137's legacy aggregate description as evidence.
Use the current Bodleian catalog's seven nested msItems unless stronger work-level evidence appears.

open_questions.question: Can the four legacy digitized-but-unavailable British Library objects be reactivated or supplied, and can complete surrogates be obtained for the other true BL witnesses?
next_steps: Planned whole-object-first route: ask whether existing complete files can be reactivated or supplied; no enquiry has yet been submitted.
If unavailable, plan a full-color whole-object request with exact target leaves identified separately.
Obtain written reuse and contract terms independently of delivery.

open_questions.question: Do MS Film 1232 or World Microfilms Section I Reel 15 preserve complete MS 853 frame coverage, and can Lambeth supply or digitize one before new photography?
next_steps: Planned whole-object-first route: ask Lambeth to verify frame inventory and completeness of both reported surrogates; no enquiry has yet been submitted.
Plan full-color whole-codex photography only if no adequate complete surrogate can be supplied.
Treat the public five-image selection and any target-only order as partial fallback coverage.

open_questions.question: Can the Bodleian supply an existing complete surrogate or create a full-color whole-codex reproduction of MS Bodley 832?
next_steps: Planned whole-object-first route: ask Special Collections whether a nonpublic complete surrogate exists; no enquiry has yet been submitted.
If not, plan a whole-codex request and identify ff. 174r–175v separately.
Obtain written reuse terms before publication or redistribution.

open_questions.question: Does Jesus College hold a complete microfilm or digital surrogate of Q.G.8, and under what supply and reuse terms?
next_steps: Planned whole-object-first route: ask the Old Library for an existing complete surrogate; no enquiry has yet been submitted.
If none exists, plan full-color whole-codex coverage with ff. 77r–78v identified separately.
Do not infer reuse permission from supply.

open_questions.question: What rights, if any, did the CME repository affirmer hold and intend to waive in inherited U-M transcription/XML data and edition-derived content?
next_steps: Seek provenance and contributor documentation from the repository maintainer and U-M.
Do not apply the software-worded Unlicense beyond rights held by its affirmer.
Continue to state U-M's current NoC-US assertion and the embedded legacy XML availability warning separately.
